answer:
As of May 2026, two of the most prominent latest agentic RAG research outputs are:
1.  **Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey on Agentic RAG** (very recent 2026 preprint):
    This is the newest comprehensive analytical survey for the field. It traces the full evolution of basic naive RAG to modern agentic RAG paradigms, introduces a formal principled taxonomy of agentic RAG architectures categorized by agent cardinality (single-agent vs multi-agent setups), and summarizes state-of-the-art implementations, public benchmarks, and unresolved open challenges for the space.
2.  Applied 2026 multimodal multi-agent GraphRAG work:
    A newly published research paper introducing a unified Generative AI platform that integrates multi-agent collaborative workflows with graph-enhanced RAG (GraphRAG). It is designed to support complex multi-step reasoning over mixed structured + unstructured data, and outperforms standard non-agentic GraphRAG on domain tasks including enterprise knowledge base querying and scientific literature synthesis.

There is also an actively updated open GitHub repository (`varinguru/Agentic-RAG-researchpapers`) that curates all newly released agentic RAG papers in real time if you want to track ongoing cutting-edge work in the area.
